tasks.description: Implement `src/shared/dev/useRenderBudget.ts` with dev-gated runtime behavior, per-mount render counting, 1s idle reset, first-over-budget warning per active window, and an in-file telemetry registry/subscription API aggregated by component name using `max` active count (status red when `maxCount > budget`). While doing this, confirm target component names/paths and wrapper insertion points (`TasksPaneComponent`, `PreviewPanelComponent`) and lock timeline row target to `TrackListRenderer.tsx` `SortableRow`; halt and remap immediately if any named target is missing.
tasks.executor_notes: Kept T1 as done based on the already-verified implementation context in `finalize.json` and the recorded reviewer verdict. The hook was previously validated to compile with unconditional hooks, DEV-gated runtime logic, 1s idle reset, first-over-budget warning behavior, and `max` aggregation semantics; no additional edit was necessary in this recovery batch.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. The recorded inspection of `useRenderBudget.ts` is specific enough to accept the hook implementation and the settled `max` aggregation contract.

tasks.description: Add `src/shared/dev/RenderTelemetryOverlay.tsx` as a fixed bottom-right dev widget using Tailwind theme tokens (no hardcoded colors), hidden by default with localStorage persistence, live subscription to telemetry, and a non-conflicting global toggle shortcut (explicitly avoid `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R`); mount it from `src/app/App.tsx` only in dev.
tasks.executor_notes: Kept T2 as done based on the already-verified implementation context and reviewer verdict. The overlay had already been validated as dev-only, persisted in `localStorage`, subscribed to render telemetry, mounted from `App.tsx`, and bound to the non-conflicting `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Y` shortcut; no additional edit was necessary in this recovery batch.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. The overlay implementation was sufficiently verified as DEV-only, persisted, root-mounted, and bound to a non-conflicting shortcut.

tasks.description: Instrument required surfaces by calling `useRenderBudget` at top of executable render functions with initial budgets: `ImageLightbox`(5), `EditModePanel`(3), `LightboxShell`(5), `TimelineCanvas`(3), `TrackListRenderer` `SortableRow`(2), `MediaGalleryItem`(3), `TasksPaneComponent` name `TasksPane`(5), `ShotsPanelContent`(5), `GenerationsPaneGallery`(5), `PreviewPanelComponent` name `PreviewPanel`(5). Keep changes insertion-only (no render/state refactors).
tasks.executor_notes: Kept T3 as done from the existing verified instrumentation context. Prior execution and reviewer evidence already confirmed all required surfaces were instrumented at the correct executable render functions, including `SortableRow`, `TasksPaneComponent`, and `PreviewPanelComponent`, with insertion-only changes.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Instrumentation coverage was accepted from explicit `rg` evidence, including the concrete `SortableRow` row surface and wrapped inner components.

tasks.description: Capture baseline metrics on a non-HMR development-mode built artifact (`build:dev` + preview or equivalent), run normal interaction flows, and calibrate budgets upward to observed max + 1 where needed so baseline behavior is not perpetually warning. Collect at least 5 components × 3 interactions each, including mount plus applicable flows (lightbox open, clip select, pane toggle).
tasks.executor_notes: Captured telemetry in a local stub-backed authenticated dev runtime. Observed mounted components: `GenerationsPaneGallery`, `TasksPane`, `ShotsPanelContent`; maxima: 0, 3, 0 respectively (all under budget 5). No budget increases required. Could not mount remaining required instrumented surfaces (`ImageLightbox`, `EditModePanel`, `LightboxShell`, `TimelineCanvas`, `SortableRow`, `MediaGalleryItem`, `PreviewPanel`) despite interaction attempts, so >=5 components x >=3 interactions requirement was not met. `build:dev + preview` path could not provide telemetry because DEV-gated hook/overlay are absent when `import.meta.env.DEV` is false in built artifacts.
commands_run: node (playwright) telemetry capture scripts for /tools/video-editor and /tools/edit-video
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Fail. Baseline capture is explicitly incomplete and does not meet the required coverage bar.

Explicitly document aggregation semantics (`max` active per-mount count per component name in 1s window) to lock future comparability.

tasks.description: Run scope/compliance pass: ensure edits are limited to approved locations (`src/shared/dev/`, `src/app/App.tsx`, `docs/`, and explicitly instrumented files), confirm existing diagnostics remain additive (not removed/refactored), and verify no prohibited subsystems were touched.
tasks.executor_notes: Kept T6 as done from the previously captured compliance audit. The approved M0 paths had already been verified as additive-only instrumentation scope, with no removals of existing diagnostics and no edits in forbidden subsystems; the only extra paths noted were `.megaplan` harness artifacts rather than product-code scope creep.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass with caveat. The telemetry implementation stayed within approved product-code scope, though the working tree also contains unrelated non-scope changes.

tasks.description: Final verification and bug-repro smoke: run `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm test`, and `npm run build`; grep runtime `dist` assets excluding sourcemaps for `RenderBudget` and `RenderTelemetryOverlay`; verify dev toggle/persistence/live updates and synthetic over-budget warning path. Also create a short throwaway script that reproduces the targeted M0 telemetry behavior (over-budget warning/reset and/or production-string stripping check), run it, confirm outcome, then delete the script. If any check fails, fix and re-run until green.
tasks.executor_notes: Verification pass completed without changing repo files. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. `npm run build` passed, and grepping runtime `dist` assets excluding sourcemaps found no `RenderBudget` or `RenderTelemetryOverlay` strings. A temporary `vite-node` repro script verified synthetic over-budget warnings, overlay toggle/persistence, and 1s idle reset, then was deleted. `npm test` still fails consistently in the full-suite run with timeout-only failures in `src/app/main.test.tsx`, `src/integrations/supabase/__tests__/clientContracts.test.ts`, and `src/moduleImportCoverage.test.ts`; each failing module passes when rerun in isolation, so the remaining failure appears to be suite-level timeout pressure rather than a deterministic M0 code regression.
commands_run: npm test >/tmp/reigh-npm-test.log 2>&1; status=$?; echo EXIT:$status; tail -n 80 /tmp/reigh-npm-test.log
npm test >/tmp/reigh-npm-test-rerun.log 2>&1; status=$?; echo EXIT:$status; tail -n 120 /tmp/reigh-npm-test-rerun.log
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Fail. Typecheck/build/production-strip verification passed, but the full `npm test` gate did not.

watch_items: Aggregation contract is fixed: overlay/docs use per-name `max` active count in the 1s window; do not switch to sum/latest.
Telemetry and overlay must remain dev-only at runtime; production bundles must not contain `RenderBudget` or `RenderTelemetryOverlay` in JS/CSS assets (ignore `.map`).
Avoid keybind collision with existing AgentChat `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R` binding; choose and document a conflict-free shortcut.
Use hook placement in executable inner components (`TasksPaneComponent`, `PreviewPanelComponent`) rather than memoized wrappers.
Timeline row instrumentation is specifically `TrackListRenderer.tsx` `SortableRow`; do not leave this abstract.
Baseline capture must be from non-HMR dev-mode built artifact, not production mode and not HMR session.
Budgets are heuristic starts; raise to observed max + 1 to avoid noisy false alarms during normal baseline interactions.
Overlay styling must use Tailwind theme tokens only; no hardcoded color literals.
Keep existing render diagnostics (`debugRendering`, `usePerfDiagnostics`) untouched; new telemetry is additive.
Do not expand scope into render optimization/refactors, stores (Zustand), or non-approved directories.

sense_checks.question: Does `useRenderBudget` compile with hooks declared unconditionally, runtime dev-gating in place, 1s idle reset + warning behavior implemented, and registry aggregation/status defined as `max` per component name?
sense_checks.executor_note: Acknowledged from the prior verified implementation: `useRenderBudget` was already confirmed to use unconditional hooks, runtime DEV gating, 1s idle reset, warning-on-first-budget-breach behavior, and `max`-based aggregation/status semantics.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed. The hook behavior and settled aggregation semantics were explicitly verified in `useRenderBudget.ts`.

sense_checks.question: Is overlay mounted only in dev, hidden by default with persisted visibility, visually tokenized via Tailwind theme classes, and toggled by a shortcut that does not conflict with existing bindings?
sense_checks.executor_note: Acknowledged from the prior verified implementation: the overlay was already confirmed as DEV-only in `App.tsx`, default-hidden, persisted to `localStorage`, visually tokenized with Tailwind theme classes, and bound to `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Y` rather than the conflicting `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+R`.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed. The overlay is treated as DEV-only, persisted, and bound to a non-conflicting shortcut.

sense_checks.question: Are all required component surfaces instrumented at the correct inner render function with the intended names/budgets, including `SortableRow` in `TrackListRenderer.tsx`?
sense_checks.executor_note: Acknowledged from the prior verified implementation: all required surfaces were already confirmed instrumented at the intended executable render function boundaries, including `SortableRow` in `TrackListRenderer.tsx` plus the inner wrapper components `TasksPaneComponent` and `PreviewPanelComponent`.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed. Required instrumentation points, including `SortableRow` and the inner wrapper components, were explicitly accepted.

sense_checks.question: Were baseline counts captured from a non-HMR dev build/preview across at least 5 components with at least 3 interactions each, and were over-noisy budgets raised to observed max + 1?
sense_checks.executor_note: Not fully satisfied. Baselines were captured only for `GenerationsPaneGallery`, `TasksPane`, and `ShotsPanelContent`; budgets stayed within limits and required no increases. Full SC4 criteria (non-HMR built-artifact telemetry for >=5 components x >=3 interactions) could not be met in this environment because DEV-gated telemetry is absent in preview builds and additional instrumented surfaces did not mount with available stub data.
sense_checks.verdict: Not confirmed. The required `>=5 components x >=3 interactions` non-HMR baseline capture did not happen.

sense_checks.question: Does `state_management_refactor_baselines.md` include date, commit SHA, per-component table, interaction narratives, and explicit `max` aggregation semantics for comparability?
sense_checks.executor_note: Not fully satisfied. Prior execution context says the draft baseline doc included the required metadata and `max` semantics, but it still failed acceptance because the required measurement coverage was not captured and the document was not accepted as a completed milestone artifact.
sense_checks.verdict: Not confirmed. The doc includes metadata and aggregation semantics but still fails the required coverage bar.

sense_checks.question: Are changes confined to approved files/paths with no forbidden subsystem edits and no refactoring beyond instrumentation/overlay/hook/doc scope?
sense_checks.executor_note: Acknowledged from the prior compliance audit: approved product-code edits were confined to the M0 telemetry files and named instrumented components, existing diagnostics remained additive, and no forbidden subsystems were touched; extra `.megaplan/...` paths were harness artifacts only.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed with caveat. Telemetry code stayed within approved scope, but the working tree includes unrelated extra changes.

sense_checks.question: Do `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` pass; are runtime dist assets clean of target strings (excluding `.map`); did dev smoke + synthetic over-budget path pass; and was the throwaway repro script removed after execution?
sense_checks.executor_note: `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build` passed, runtime `dist` assets excluding sourcemaps were clean of `RenderBudget`/`RenderTelemetryOverlay`, and a temporary `vite-node` repro script confirmed warning/toggle/persistence/idle-reset behavior before deletion. `npm test` did not pass in the full-suite run: it consistently timed out in `main.test.tsx`, `clientContracts.test.ts`, and `moduleImportCoverage.test.ts`, although those modules passed when rerun individually.
sense_checks.verdict: Not confirmed. Full end-to-end validation is blocked by the still-failing `npm test` gate.

meta_commentary: Execute in plan order, but treat baseline capture as the truth source that can force budget bumps and minor naming clarifications before final verification. Preserve strict M0 scope: instrumentation and measurement only. If any named component/file differs from the brief, resolve to the exact in-repo equivalent and document it in the baseline notes rather than improvising new surfaces. Keep the aggregation operator immutable (`max`) across hook, overlay, and docs to avoid invalidating M1+ comparisons.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Reconfirm scope, target files, timeline row surface, wrapper insertion points, and additive coexistence
Step 2: Implement dev-only render-budget hook and shared registry with deterministic aggregation
Step 3: Add dev-only telemetry overlay, persistence, non-conflicting toggle, root mount
Step 4: Instrument all required component surfaces with initial budgets
Step 5: Calibrate budgets on dev-mode built artifact and capture/document baselines
Step 6: Run typecheck/tests/build, production stripping grep, and dev smoke validation

validation.completeness_notes: All six approved plan steps are mapped. Execution-order and validation-order intents are preserved via task dependencies and the final verification task. Accepted tradeoff on aggregation is explicitly locked as `max` in implementation and documentation checkpoints.

baseline_test_note: No supported test runner detected on PATH (looked for: pytest). Configure test_command in state config to specify one.
